text:
There was a little old-fashioned cottage nestled among thickets of blackberry bushes. You could hear music bouncing off those branches. Inside the cottage sat an old woman, humming softly.

One bright spring eve, she stopped humming. As her eyes fluttered upwards, they settled on a flock of starlings darting around a blooming garden. They swooped gracefully, casting long shadows. Just as the last olive leaf vanished into the twilight, a whistle pierced the night air.

The old woman stood up, her round glasses perched on her nose. Slowly, she walked toward the window. Peering out, she found herself surrounded by tiny silhouettes dancing around the bush. She closed the window, her curiosity piqued.

That night, she heard her first song ever.

It was a song about love. Unusual, yet enchantingly so. There were no words. Just music. The night filled with it. By dawn, she'd composed her own.

Every year thereafter,
